In this game, Black successfully equalized and eventually gained a slight advantage through dynamic play, exploiting White's over-extension and misplaced pieces. Black's pawn structure and active piece play (e.g., the bishop on c8 and knight on f6) created long-term pressure. White's last moves (e.g., pawn pushes and piece retreats) failed to counter Black's initiative effectively.
